summary: Reworked the baselib objalloc statistics helpers so the inline accessors operate directly on HSD_ObjAllocData instead of an HSD_ObjAllocInfo wrapper, and updated HSD_ObjDumpStat to keep its local reporting table as function-pointer plus label entries. This removed the public HSD_ObjAllocInfo typedef from objalloc.h and changed call sites to explicitly call each GetAllocData function before reading using/freed/peak counters. A small VS Code file association update added xstring as C.
what_changed: Removed the HSD_ObjAllocInfo typedef from src/sysdolphin/baselib/objalloc.h.
Renamed/replaced inline helpers HSD_ObjAllocUsed, HSD_ObjAllocFree, and HSD_ObjAllocPeak with HSD_ObjAllocGetUsing, HSD_ObjAllocGetFreed, and HSD_ObjAllocGetPeak.
Changed the objalloc inline helpers to accept HSD_ObjAllocData* directly instead of accepting a wrapper that contains a getData function pointer.
Updated HSD_ObjDumpStat in initialize.c to use a local anonymous struct array with fields HSD_ObjAllocData* (*func)(void) and char* label.
Changed the HSD_ObjDumpStat loop from pointer iteration over HSD_ObjAllocInfo entries to integer-indexed iteration over the local types array.
Updated OSReport arguments in HSD_ObjDumpStat to call each allocation data function explicitly, then pass its returned HSD_ObjAllocData* into the new accessor helpers.
Added xstring to .vscode/settings.json C file associations.
smart_moves: Kept the reporting table local to HSD_ObjDumpStat instead of exposing a generic HSD_ObjAllocInfo type in the shared objalloc header.
Made the objalloc accessors simpler and more direct: each helper now validates and reads an HSD_ObjAllocData* rather than hiding an internal getData callback invocation.
Preserved existing HSD_ASSERT line IDs for the three accessors while changing their parameter type, which suggests care was taken not to disturb assert identity more than necessary.
Used a sentinel { NULL, NULL } entry and a label-null loop condition to keep the local objalloc reporting list compact.
decomp_lessons: Avoid adding public header structs solely to support one local reporting table when an anonymous local struct can express the same data more narrowly.

For matching or decomp cleanup, helper signatures should model the actual data being accessed when possible; here using HSD_ObjAllocData* directly reduced indirection and removed a questionable wrapper type.
When many allocation subsystems share the same stats fields, use common accessors over the shared data struct and keep per-subsystem GetAllocData functions in the caller-side table.
Header changes in decomp projects should be conservative: removing an unnecessary typedef can reduce false API surface and prevent future code from depending on invented abstractions.
naming_conventions: Accessor names use the HSD_ObjAllocGetX form: HSD_ObjAllocGetUsing, HSD_ObjAllocGetFreed, HSD_ObjAllocGetPeak.
The stat labels in HSD_ObjDumpStat remain short lowercase strings matching baselib object categories: aobj, fobj, id, slist, dlist, vec, mtx, robj, rval, shadow, render, chan, tevreg.
Per-type allocation data providers follow HSD_<Type>GetAllocData naming, such as HSD_AObjGetAllocData, HSD_FObjGetAllocData, and HSD_TevRegGetAllocData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff replaces a named HSD_ObjAllocInfo array and pointer-based loop with a local anonymous struct array and int-indexed loop, a common tactic when generated code shape depends on whether the compiler sees a local aggregate type and indexed access.
Calling types[i].func() directly at each accessor argument may better match expected call ordering/codegen than burying the function call inside an inline helper that takes a wrapper pointer.
Reducing the inline helper body to assert plus field read makes the helpers less likely to introduce unwanted loads from an info wrapper before the actual HSD_ObjAllocData access.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching HSD_ObjAllocGetUsing or objalloc.h to see whether the direct HSD_ObjAllocData accessor pattern was reused.
Search for remaining references to HSD_ObjAllocInfo, HSD_ObjAllocUsed, HSD_ObjAllocFree, or HSD_ObjAllocPeak to confirm this PR fully removed the old wrapper API.
Compare HSD_ObjDumpStat assembly before and after this PR if investigating why the local anonymous struct and indexed loop were needed.
